newsSince: No new material company-specific catalyst since June 9. Q1 2026 results (revenue $22.4B, +16% YoY) were April; robotaxi service expanded to Dallas/Houston with paid miles roughly doubling sequentially; Optimus large-scale factory prep begun at Fremont with a next-gen unveiling targeted for late-July/August; 2026 capex raised to >$25B. All pre-window, so no fresh fundamental signal for the robotics ranking.

sources.label: Tesla signals >$25B capex, Optimus late-Jul/Aug, robotaxi expansion (Seeking Alpha)

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens: Tesla is the obvious, not hidden, humanoid name. No new in-window catalyst (Optimus unveiling still targeted late-July/Aug; robotaxi expansion already known). At $1.45T cap with -12% YTD, generic humanoid beta still outweighs hidden-alpha purity. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Largest-scale humanoid effort with a dedicated Fremont Optimus line and a late-summer next-gen unveiling that could re-rate the robotics narrative.
verdicts.neutral: Essential humanoid infrastructure exposure but fully obvious and richly valued; robotaxi progress real but priced as option value.
verdicts.bear: $1.45T cap leaves little hidden alpha; Optimus commercialization unproven and EV core under pressure (-12% YTD).
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Late-July/August next-gen Optimus unveiling and first large-scale production line.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Optimus/robotaxi commercialization slips while valuation stays demanding.
